Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to answer Provide a DuckDB SQL query that selects events from the table event_log excluding cases that meet the criteria containing the specific three activity sequence in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order with the time between Create Order and Confirm Order greater than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to produce a final result set of all events from the cases that do NOT match the criteria The query must be written in DuckDB SQL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must consider the direct succession requirement these three events appear consecutively with no other events of the same case in between

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Simplify Use window functions to assign a row number per case ordered by timestamp Then join events with LEAD to get next activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For three consecutive events we need e1 e2 e3 consecutive Use LAG and LEAD to get activity of previous and next events Or use LEAD2

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Use subquery with window functions SELECT case_id activity timestamp LEAD activity OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_activity LEAD activity 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_next_activity LEAD timestamp OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_timestamp LEAD timestamp 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_next_timestamp FROM event_log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we can filter for rows where activity Approve Purchase Requisition AND next_activity Create Order AND next_next_activity Confirm Order AND next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp greater than interval 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But careful The time elapsed between the Create Order event and the Confirm Order event is the difference between the timestamps of those two events next_timestamp and next_next_timestamp So we need to compute next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp greater than interval 5 days

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus we can find case_ids that satisfy this condition Then exclude them

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: With sequence_cases as select distinct case_id from subquery where activity Approve Purchase Requisition and next_activity Create Order and next_next_activity Confirm Order and next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp greater than interval 5 days Then select all from event_log where not exists matching case_id order by case_id timestamp

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Edge Cases Might be multiple events with same timestamp but ordering by timestamp only If there are ties row_number may be needed to break ties But typical process logs have distinct timestamps per event

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can refine by using ROW_NUMBER with ordered as select all and row_number over partition by case_id order by timestamp activity as rn from event_log then join ordered rows rn rn plus 1 and rn plus 2 and filter on the three activities and timestamp difference greater than interval 5 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Using DISTINCT case_id ensures that if any occurrence meets criteria the whole case is excluded

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Better to provide simpler LEAD version
